description_long: This is a military attack airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and twin turbofan engines mounted on pylons above the rear fuselage. The fuselage is robust and features a bubble canopy cockpit positioned at the front, providing excellent visibility. The aircraft is equipped with multiple hardpoints under the wings for carrying ordnance such as missiles and bombs, and the nose section is slightly elongated, likely housing avionics or weaponry. The tail assembly consists of a twin vertical stabilizer connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle type, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear under the forward fuselage.
description: A military attack airplane with high-mounted straight wings, twin rear turbofan engines, a bubble canopy cockpit, and an H-tail with twin vertical stabilizers.
description_view_0: Rear view showing twin turbofan engines mounted above the fuselage and the H-tail with twin vertical stabilizers.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the bubble canopy cockpit, straight high-mounted wings with visible ordnance, and the robust fuselage.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wide wingspan, cockpit canopy, and underwing hardpoints for ordnance.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, engine nacelles, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with multiple hardpoints and no visible winglets; slight anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan engines mounted above the rear fuselage on pylons.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all appearing retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed bubble canopy cockpit with apparent multi-seat configuration.
